finding.summary: High true peak relative to sample peak indicates significant intersample peaks, leaving only 5.5 dBTP headroom. With 88% silence, the RMS and integrated LUFS are unreliable, but the peak levels are accurate.
finding.probable_cause: The raw kick hit was recorded/generated with high peak level and no limiting; the intersample overshoot exceeds the sample peak by 3.5 dB, which can cause distortion when the signal is converted or further processed.
finding.confidence_reason: Confidence is capped at low because audio.silence_fraction is 0.880.

evidence_refs.description: True peak level -5.5 dBFS (3.5 dB higher than sample peak)
88% silence dilutes RMS and LUFS, but peaks remain valid
Crest factor 12 dB is inflated by silence; actual crest factor during hits is lower

proposal.reason: Reducing track volume by 3 dB will lower both sample peak and true peak, increasing headroom and reducing risk of intersample-peak distortion during further processing or conversion.
